SEARCH
Package edu.yu.cs.com3810.coordinator

Class Coordinator

java.lang.Object
edu.yu.cs.com3810.coordinator.Coordinator
All Implemented Interfaces:
ICoordinator
public class Coordinator extends Object implements ICoordinator
Concrete implementation of ICoordinator.

Each instance represents one node in the coordinator cluster. On startup, all nodes participate in leader election. Only the leader processes incoming API requests and publishes commands; follower nodes shadow all state updates via their message bus subscriptions so they are ready to take over instantly.

  • Constructor Details

    • Coordinator

      public Coordinator(String nodeId, List<String> peerNodeIds, MessageBusClientRunner busClient)
      Constructs a Coordinator node.
      Parameters:
      nodeId - unique identifier for this node within the cluster
      peerNodeIds - IDs of all other coordinator nodes in the cluster
      busClient - the Message Bus client for publishing and subscribing

  • Method Details

    • start

      public void start()
      Starts this coordinator node: registers with the message bus, subscribes to relevant topics (REGISTER, HEARTBEAT, COMMAND results), and initiates the leader election process.

      Registers this node with the message bus, subscribes to REGISTER, HEARTBEAT, and COMMAND topics, then calls startElection().

      Specified by:
      start in interface ICoordinator

    • stop

      public void stop()
      Gracefully shuts down this coordinator node. Unregisters from the message bus and notifies peers.
      Specified by:
      stop in interface ICoordinator

    • startElection

      public void startElection()
      Starts a new leader election by launching a LeaderElectionRunner in a dedicated thread.

      Called on startup and by HeartbeatMonitor when the leader's heartbeat timeout elapses. No-op if an election thread is already running.

      Creates a new LeaderElectionRunner with this node's ID, the peer node list, and the shared bus client, then starts it in a new thread named "leader-election-<nodeId>". Stores the thread and runner references so receiveOk(String) can forward OK signals to the active runner. If electionThread is already alive, returns immediately without starting a second election.

      Specified by:
      startElection in interface ICoordinator

    • receiveElection

      public void receiveElection(String fromNodeId)
      Called by the ELECTION topic subscriber when an ELECTION message arrives from a lower-priority peer node.

      Per the Bully Algorithm, this node must:

      1. Reply with an OK message targeted at fromNodeId.
      2. Call ICoordinator.startElection() if not already running one, since this node has higher priority and should assert leadership.

      Publishes an OK message back to fromNodeId via the bus client (targeted ElectionMessage with type OK and targetNodeId = fromNodeId), then calls startElection() to assert this node's higher priority.

      Specified by:
      receiveElection in interface ICoordinator
      Parameters:
      fromNodeId - the node ID that sent the ELECTION message

    • receiveOk

      public void receiveOk(String fromNodeId)
      Called by the ELECTION topic subscriber when an OK message arrives targeted at this node.

      Signals the active LeaderElectionRunner that a higher-priority node is alive via electionRunner.receiveOk(), causing it to stand down.

      Forwards the signal to the active LeaderElectionRunner via electionRunner.receiveOk(fromNodeId) so the runner stands down after its timeout. If no election is currently running (electionRunner is null), this call is a no-op.

      Specified by:
      receiveOk in interface ICoordinator
      Parameters:
      fromNodeId - the node ID of the higher-priority peer that sent OK

    • receiveCoordinator

      public void receiveCoordinator(String newLeaderId)
      Called by the ELECTION topic subscriber when a COORDINATOR broadcast arrives.

      Updates this node's local currentLeaderId to newLeaderId and cancels any in-progress election thread. If this node is the new leader, activates leader-only responsibilities: starts the AutomationHub thread and begins accepting requests from the API Gateway.

      Sets currentLeaderId = newLeaderId. If newLeaderId equals this node's own ID, transitions this node into leader mode: activates the AutomationHub thread and marks the node ready to accept requests from the API Gateway. If this node was previously leader and is now being superseded, deactivates leader-only responsibilities. In all cases, clears electionThread and electionRunner.

      Specified by:
      receiveCoordinator in interface ICoordinator
      Parameters:
      newLeaderId - the node ID of the newly elected leader

    • getCurrentLeaderId

      public String getCurrentLeaderId()
      Returns the node ID of the currently elected leader coordinator. All nodes — leader and follower — maintain this value. The API Gateway calls this on any coordinator node to discover where to route requests.

      Returns the value of currentLeaderId, set by the most recent receiveCoordinator(String) call. Returns null if no election has completed yet — the API Gateway polls until this is non-null.

      Specified by:
      getCurrentLeaderId in interface ICoordinator
      Returns:
      the node ID of the current leader, or null if no election has completed yet

    • isLeader

      public boolean isLeader()
      Returns whether this coordinator instance is the current leader.

      Returns nodeId.equals(currentLeaderId).

      Specified by:
      isLeader in interface ICoordinator
      Returns:
      true if this node's ID equals currentLeaderId

    • registerDevice

      public void registerDevice(Device device)
      Handles a device registration event received from the REGISTER topic. Creates a Device record and stores it in the local registry. All coordinator nodes call this so every node maintains full state.

      Called whenever a RegistrationPayload arrives on the REGISTER topic. Converts the payload into a Device and stores it.

      Specified by:
      registerDevice in interface ICoordinator
      Parameters:
      device - the device that has registered itself

    • getAllDevices

      public List<Device> getAllDevices()
      Returns all devices currently registered with the system.
      Specified by:
      getAllDevices in interface ICoordinator
      Returns:
      a list of all known Device instances

    • getDeviceById

      public Device getDeviceById(String deviceId)
      Returns a specific registered device by its ID.
      Specified by:
      getDeviceById in interface ICoordinator
      Parameters:
      deviceId - the ID of the device to look up
      Returns:
      the Device with the given ID, or null if not found

    • handleCommand

      public CommandStatus handleCommand(Command command)
      Processes a user-initiated command request. Validates the device and action, publishes the command to the COMMAND topic on the message bus, and returns the resulting status once the device responds.

      Only the current leader should call this method.

      Validates that the target device exists and supports the requested action before publishing. Blocks until a result is received on the COMMAND topic or a timeout elapses.

      Specified by:
      handleCommand in interface ICoordinator
      Parameters:
      command - the command to dispatch
      Returns:
      the CommandStatus after the command has been processed

    • handleCommandResult

      public void handleCommandResult(String commandId, CommandStatus status)
      Handles the result of a previously issued command received from the message bus. Updates the command's status and forwards the result to the API Gateway.
      Specified by:
      handleCommandResult in interface ICoordinator
      Parameters:
      commandId - the ID of the command whose result was received
      status - the outcome of the command execution

    • addAutomationRule

      public void addAutomationRule(AutomationRule rule)
      Stores a new automation rule and forwards it to the Automation Hub. Only the current leader should call this method.
      Specified by:
      addAutomationRule in interface ICoordinator
      Parameters:
      rule - the automation rule to add

    • removeAutomationRule

      public void removeAutomationRule(String ruleId)
      Removes an existing automation rule by its ID and notifies the Automation Hub. Only the current leader should call this method.
      Specified by:
      removeAutomationRule in interface ICoordinator
      Parameters:
      ruleId - the ID of the rule to remove

    • handleDeviceHeartbeat

      public void handleDeviceHeartbeat(String deviceId)
      Processes a heartbeat message received from an edge device. Updates the device's last-seen timestamp and marks it as online.
      Specified by:
      handleDeviceHeartbeat in interface ICoordinator
      Parameters:
      deviceId - the ID of the device that sent the heartbeat

    • handleNodeHeartbeat

      public void handleNodeHeartbeat(String nodeId)
      Processes a heartbeat received from another coordinator node. Used to detect leader failure. If the leader's heartbeat is missed, triggers a new leader election.

      If the heartbeat is from the current leader and the leader is not this node, resets the leader-failure timer. If no heartbeat is received within the configured timeout window, calls startElection().

      Specified by:
      handleNodeHeartbeat in interface ICoordinator
      Parameters:
      nodeId - the ID of the coordinator node that sent the heartbeat
